text: An API, or Application Programming Interface, allows different software programs to communicate with each other. When working with APIs, you typically make HTTP requests to a server and receive data in JSON format. JSON is a lightweight way to structure data, similar to a list or dictionary. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the data. It’s also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, you should include error handling in your code to manage problems like network issues or invalid responses.
